arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5; which dotnet 2>/dev/null

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in the backtrace. The key frames are from `libcoreclr.so` with BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

To symbolicate these, I need to:
1. Find the debug symbols for this specific build of `libcoreclr.so`
2. Use them to resolve the PC offsets to function names

The BuildId is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. 

This is a .NET runtime library for Android arm64. I can try Microsoft's symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/` to fetch the debug symbols, using the standard ELF BuildId path format to construct the download URL.

The BuildId is `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`. I'll attempt to retrieve the symbols using curl with the proper ELF symbol server URL pattern for libcoreclr.so.

For ELF binaries, the Microsoft symbol server expects the format with the BuildId embedded in the path. I can try downloading the debug symbols directly, then use tools like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line` to resolve the addresses. Let me first check what tools are available on the system, then decide whether to install `dotnet-symbol` or work with the available symbolization tools.

arguments.command: which llvm-symbolizer addr2line llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5; which dotnet 2>/dev/null

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
BUILDID="4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801"
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# ELF debug symbol format
curl -sSL -o /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so.dbg \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1

echo "---"

# Also try the main binary with symbols
curl -sSL -o /tmp/symbols/libcoreclr.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libcoreclr.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libcoreclr.so" \
  -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
Good, we have `addr2line` and `dotnet`. Let me try to download the debug symbols for `libcoreclr.so` from Microsoft's symbol server using the BuildId `4ca7e2f19b3d605847a1c8e3f5b7d9a2c4e6f801`.

Let me try both the binary and the debug symbols from the symbol server.

arguments.command:
# Try installing dotnet-symbol tool
dotnet tool install -g dotnet-symbol 2>&1 | tail -3
export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/.dotnet/tools"
# Try to download symbols using dotnet-symbol
